text.title: WireGuard VPN Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the WireGuard VPN methodology. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Each peer's AllowedIPs list decides which packets route to that peer. NEVER set AllowedIPs=0.0.0.0/0 on the server for a single client (that breaks every other client's routing).
rule.rationale: WireGuard does cryptokey routing — overlapping AllowedIPs cause silent black-holing.

rule.statement: net.ipv4.ip_forward=1 (and net.ipv6.conf.all.forwarding=1 for IPv6) MUST be set in /etc/sysctl.d/99-wireguard.conf and active. Server-only NAT mode requires it.
rule.rationale: Without forwarding, packets from the tunnel don't egress; clients connect but reach nothing.

rule.statement: Private keys MUST be chmod 600 and NEVER committed. wg0.conf containing PrivateKey is gitignored.
rule.rationale: Private key compromise = MITM of the tunnel; pre-commit hook must catch the pattern.

rule.statement: VpnPlan names a single owner. 'team' / 'we' / '' are forbidden.
Mobile / NAT-traversal peers MUST set PersistentKeepalive=25 (seconds). Other peers omit it.
rule.rationale: NAT mapping expires; keepalive holds the punch-hole open for inbound packets to mobile.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent's context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-wireguard-vpn.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
